fault_hypothesis: On line 60, the mean is computed by dividing total by `window` (the maximum window size) instead of dividing by `len(recent)` (the actual number of samples). When fewer samples exist than the window, this produces an incorrect (too-small) mean.

constraints.intent: The computed mean must equal total divided by the actual sample count, not by window size.

spec.expr: len(recent__1) == window or abs(mean__1 * len(recent__1) - total__1) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: After computing mean, mean * len(recent) should equal total when samples < window.

spec.expr: total__1 / len(recent__1) == total__1 / window or len(recent__1) == window

constraints.intent: Before using mean, dividing by window is only correct when len(recent) equals window.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result - round(sum(_extract_recent_window(values, window)) / len(_extract_recent_window(values, window)), _PRECISION_DIGITS)) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: The returned mean should be based on the count of actual samples, not the window size.
